sections.instruction: Questions 11-15: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER. Questions 16-18: Choose THREE letters A-G. Questions 19 and 20: Choose TWO letters A-E.

questions.content:

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 25-27: Choose THREE letters A-G. Questions 28-30: Complete the sentences below. Write ONE WORD OR A NUMBER.

questions.content: The tutor thinks that Sandra’s proposal
options: A should be re-ordered in some parts
C ought to include more information

questions.content: The proposal would be easier to follow if Sandra
What was the problem with the formatting on Sandra’s proposal?
options: A Separate points were not clearly identified
B The headings were not always clear
C Page numbering was not used in an appropriate way

questions.content: Sandra became interested in visiting the Navajo National Park through

C photographs she found on the internet

questions.content: Which THREE topics does Sandra agree to include in the proposal?

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.
sections.passage_or_context:
GEOGRAPHY
Studying geography helps us to understand:
• The effects of different processes on the {{BLANK}} of the Earth
• The dynamic between {{BLANK}} and population

Two main branches of study:
• Physical features
• Human lifestyles and their {{BLANK}}

Specific study areas:
• Biophysical, topographic, political, social, economic, historical and {{BLANK}} geography and also cartography

Key point:
• Geography helps us to understand our surroundings and the associated {{BLANK}}

What do the geographers do?
• Find data e.g. conduct censuses, collect information in the form of {{BLANK}} using computer and satellite technology
• Analyse data – identify {{BLANK}} e.g. cause and effect
• Publish findings in the form of:

Maps
Can show physical features of large and small areas
But a two-dimensional map will always have some {{BLANK}}

Aerial photos
Can show vegetation problems {{BLANK}} density, ocean, floor etc.

Landsat pictures sent to receiving stations
